questions.question_text: You have taken the wrong road and must turn around. What do you do?
You have caught up with the slowly moving car ahead. What do you do?
What does the police officer's hand signal mean?
You are driving at about 70 km/h. What do you do?
You are looking for a parking space nearby. What do you do now?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You are driving at about 60 km/h and want to enter the motorway to the left. There is no traffic in your blind spot. What do you do?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You have reduced speed to 20 to 30 km/h because the lane narrows. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h and must continue straight ahead. What do you do?
You are about to catch up with the driver ahead at about 60 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 50 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. The oncoming truck is about to turn right into the side road. What do you do?
What should you pay particular attention to here?
You are driving at about 50 km/h and the driver behind has caught up with you. What do you do?
You are about to move off from a parking space. What should you pay particular attention to?
You are driving at about 40 km/h and must continue straight ahead. What do you do here?
You want to turn left at this intersection. What should you pay particular attention to?
You are about to enter a motorway, but the driver ahead is hesitating about merging. There is no traffic behind you in the nearest motorway lane. What do you do?
The driver has got out of the grey car here. Which rules apply here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for up to 3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping in the viaduct is prohibited.

questions.question_text: You have caught up with the slowly moving lorry, and you want to get ahead quickly. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You need to continue straight ahead at the intersection. What do you do?
You are driving at about 20 to 30 km/h. What do you do?
You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.

questions.question_text: You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
answers.3: Stopping and parking are prohibited here.
